steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Calculate area of square S
steps.content: Square S has a side length of 4 cm. Using the formula for the area of a square, Area = (side)², we get: $$A_{\text{square}} = 4^2 = 16\text{ cm}^2$$

steps.title: Add both areas for total
steps.content: Triangle R has area 80 cm² and square S has area 16 cm². The total area is: $$A_{\text{total}} = 80 + 16 = 96\text{ cm}^2$$ That is the correct answer.
